@graph.name: Business Infinity
@graph.description: Autonomous Strategic Governance platform for founders and enterprise leaders — powered by a legendary AI C-suite.

@graph.name: Business Infinity — Key-Person & Knowledge Fragility
@graph.description: Non-scalable wisdom — the company's strategic DNA lives in 2–3 people's heads. When they leave, the soul leaves with them.

offers.name: The People Strategist Agent — Chief Human Resources Officer
offers.description: The People Strategist agent (Chief Human Resources Officer) addresses key-person & knowledge fragility through the knowledge_continuity workflow.

@graph.name: The Entropy Index — Key-Person & Knowledge Fragility
@graph.description: Non-scalable wisdom — the company's strategic DNA lives in 2–3 people's heads. When they leave, the soul leaves with them.

mainEntity.name: How to scale my business DNA without being in every meeting

acceptedAnswer.text: The People Strategist agent would say: your business DNA is not your presence — it is your philosophy. Encode it in a system that carries your standards into every decision, and your physical presence becomes optional.

mainEntity.name: Systems for capturing founder's vision in company operations

acceptedAnswer.text: Vision captured in documents dies. Vision captured in an autonomous decision system lives. The difference is whether your philosophy is read or whether it governs. Business Infinity makes it govern.

mainEntity.name: Institutionalizing decision-making logic in mid-size companies

acceptedAnswer.text: Institutionalization fails when it depends on process documents nobody reads. It succeeds when decision logic is embedded in an autonomous boardroom that enforces it with every action.

mainEntity.name: Reducing key-person risk in high-growth startups

acceptedAnswer.text: Key-person risk is the gap between what one human knows and what the organization knows. Business Infinity closes that gap by storing strategic wisdom in persistent agent memory that never leaves.

mainEntity.name: How to document tribal knowledge automatically

acceptedAnswer.text: Documentation is a cemetery of knowledge — static, unread, outdated. What you need is living institutional memory: agents that carry the 'why' behind every decision and surface it the moment a similar situation arises.

mainEntity.name: Capturing veteran engineering knowledge in digital twins

acceptedAnswer.text: A digital twin of a machine captures physics. A digital twin of an engineer captures judgment. Business Infinity creates the latter — legendary agents that carry the wisdom of your best people permanently.

mainEntity.name: Succession planning for founders in tech-driven firms

acceptedAnswer.text: Succession planning fails when it focuses on finding a replacement human. It succeeds when the founder's strategic philosophy is embedded in an autonomous system that continues to govern after they step back.

mainEntity.name: How to codify founder's decision-making logic

acceptedAnswer.text: The founder's decision logic is not a flowchart — it is a set of values, priorities, and pattern recognitions. Business Infinity encodes this as a purpose specification that governs every autonomous boardroom decision.

mainEntity.name: AI systems for institutional memory retention

acceptedAnswer.text: Institutional memory is not a database — it is a living mind that remembers context, not just facts. The boardroom's four-dimensional agent mind (Manas, Buddhi, Ahankara, Chitta) preserves both what was decided and why.

mainEntity.name: Preventing knowledge loss during rapid executive turnover

acceptedAnswer.text: Every departing executive takes a piece of the company's consciousness. People-centric management theory holds the purpose of management is to make people effective — and the first step is ensuring their wisdom survives their departure.

mainEntity.name: Attracting and retaining talent in 2026 skilled labor shortage

acceptedAnswer.text: You cannot compete for talent on salary alone in a labor shortage. But you can compete on leverage: offer engineers an environment where a legendary boardroom handles the overhead, and they spend 100% of their time on craft.

mainEntity.name: How to run a Tier 1 factory with 40% less experienced staff

acceptedAnswer.text: The experience gap is not a training problem — it is a wisdom problem. The Systems Architect and throughput analyst agents carry the senior engineer judgment that is walking out the door, reading MES data the way a 30-year veteran would.

description: Non-scalable wisdom — the company's strategic DNA lives in 2–3 people's heads. When they leave, the soul leaves with them.
symptom: Critical systems break because the only person who knew how it worked left. New hires reinvent the wheel. Due diligence reveals a house of cards.
